sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
I see only 3 N crosses now (at cols 41-44). The upper panel crosses (New-Middle at 29-32 and Right at 35-38) are gone! They merged! But the result shows 3 N crosses, all in the lower panel area. Wait, let me look more carefully.
The N nodes are:
- (18,41)-(21,44) - This is in the upper panel's row range but at cols 41-44
- (24,41)-(27,44) - Lower-top
- (36,41)-(39,44) - Lower-bottom
So the New-Middle and Right crosses merged into a new cross at (18,41)-(21,44), which is in the upper panel but at the rightmost column position (same as Lower-top's columns). This makes sense - the merge position is determined by the g marker position when the selected cross was last activated.
So the merge DID work! The sequence was:
1. Click on New-Middle (col 30) → New-Middle selected
2. Click on col 42 (g marker position) → This triggered the merge!
3. Click on col 33 → This just selected Right (but Right no longer exists, so it selected nothing or something else)
Wait, but the merge happened after step 2, not step 3. Let me reconsider. When I clicked on col 42 (the g marker position when New-Middle was selected), the merge happened. So the pattern is:
1. Select cross A
2. Click on g marker position → Merge A with its neighbor, new cross appears at g marker position
This is different from what I thought earlier. The successful merge at step 27 was:
1. Click on Left → Left selected
2. Click on Middle → Middle selected
3. Click on Left → Left selected
4. Click on col 30 (g marker position) → Reset
5. Click on col 21 → Merge
But now I see that clicking on the g marker position (col 42) directly triggered the merge. So maybe the pattern is simpler:
1. Select cross A
2. Click on g marker position → Merge A with its neighbor
The earlier "reset" behavior might have been because I clicked on the g marker position when the wrong cross was selected, or there was some other condition.
Now I have 3 N crosses:
- New cross at (18,41)-(21,44) - upper panel, right side
- Lower-top at (24,41)-(27,44)
- Lower-bottom at (36,41)-(39,44)
The next merge would be between the new cross and Lower-top (they're vertically adjacent). Let me try:
1. Select the new cross (col 42)
2. Check g marker position
3. Click on g marker position → Merge
But wait, I need to figure out where the g marker position is when the new cross is selected. Let me first select the new cross and check.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Select the new cross at (18,41)-(21,44) and check g markers
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 19, 'col': 42}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
